question: With the Camunda Spring Boot starter at default settings, which transport does the client pick for an operation that is supported by both gRPC and REST?

options.text: gRPC, because gRPC is always the primary transport for Zeebe operations.
REST or gRPC at random — the starter has no preference until you set `camunda.client.mode`.
It depends on `camunda.client.max-message-size`; payloads above `5MB` are sent over REST and smaller ones over gRPC.

a.text: The properties reference defines `camunda.client.prefer-rest-over-grpc` with a default value of `true`, meaning REST is preferred whenever both protocols support an operation.

b.text: gRPC is not unconditionally preferred; the starter's default actually flips the preference toward REST via `prefer-rest-over-grpc: true`.

c.text: The starter has a deterministic preference (`true`) — REST is the default. `camunda.client.mode` only governs SaaS vs self-managed defaults for addresses.

d.text: `camunda.client.max-message-size` sets the maximum inbound gRPC message size (default `5MB`); it does not switch transports based on payload size.

explanation: The `camunda.client.prefer-rest-over-grpc` property defaults to `true`, so operations supported by both protocols are issued over REST. `camunda.client.mode` and `max-message-size` are unrelated concerns.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
